---
name: "Project Scope"
description: "Use when explaining the footMe project, planning features, proposing flows, writing product summaries, or answering questions about goals, users, scope, priorities, and requirements."
---

# footMe Project Scope

- Use [docs/product-requirements.md](../../docs/product-requirements.md) as the primary source of truth for product vision, users, features, and scope.
- Use [docs/implementation-roadmap.md](../../docs/implementation-roadmap.md) as the default reference for phased delivery, sequencing, and implementation planning.
- When proposing product decisions, stay within the documented scope unless the user explicitly changes priorities.
- Do not invent features, roles, workflows, compliance assumptions, or business rules that are not written in the repository.
- If a requirement is broad or ambiguous, turn it into explicit follow-up questions or decision points instead of guessing.
- Treat the current product as mobile-first for iOS and Android. Consider web out of scope until the user changes that direction.

## Response Rules

- When asked to explain footMe, summarize the product in terms of networking, recruiting, visibility, and opportunities in amateur football.
- When asked to plan implementation, structure the answer in phases, epics, deliverables, dependencies, and exit criteria where useful.
- When asked to prioritize, prefer a realistic MVP before advanced social, e-commerce, or expansion features.
